_note: ORIGINAL content. Topic coverage was checked against javascript.info and Eloquent JavaScript. No prose, examples, or exercises were copied; the references are curriculum guides only.
title: Async & Promises
summary: Work with results that arrive later.
refresher: A Promise is a placeholder for a result that is not ready yet. `async` marks a function that works with Promises. `await` waits inside that function until the Promise finishes, while the rest of the browser can keep working.

blocks.body: Some work takes time. A request to a server, a timer, or a file read may finish later. JavaScript starts that work, then keeps the page responsive while it waits for the result.

`await` lets you write those steps in a readable order. It only works inside an `async` function, or in a module that supports top-level await. If the Promise fails, JavaScript treats that failure like an error on the await line.

blocks.caption: The function waits for loadUser to settle, then prints either the returned name or a controlled failure message.
Fetch returns a Promise, not the final user object. Await both the response and the later body-parsing Promise.

blocks.body: Starting order and finishing order are not always the same. The function pauses at await, other browser work can happen, and then the function continues when the Promise is done.

blocks.title: Fetch does not reject every bad response
blocks.body: A 404 or 500 response usually still gives you a response object. Check `response.ok` before you treat the body like successful data.

blocks.prompt: What does an async function always return?
blocks.why: JavaScript wraps a returned value in a fulfilled Promise, or returns a rejected Promise when the function throws.

blocks.prompt: What happens when an awaited Promise rejects?
choices: An error is thrown at the await expression
blocks.why: Rejection enters the surrounding catch just like a synchronous throw, or rejects the async function when it is not caught.

blocks.title: Async & Promises checklist
items: Await the step that gives you the value you need.
Use try/catch where you can recover.
Do not make unrelated requests wait on each other unless they have to.
